evidence_spans.span: The forward Euler method is applied to an initial value problem by advancing from the current approximation along the current vector-field slope. Its update is y_{n+1} = y_n + hF(t_n,y_n). The step size h sets the spacing between consecutive numerical time points.

results.rationale: The step size is a parameter used in numerical methods applied to initial value problems, as shown in the forward Euler update formula where h appears. Thus, step size is applied in the context of solving initial value problems.
